steps.title: Set y equals zero and identify all roots
steps.content:
To find x-intercepts, set $y = 0$:
$$2(x-d)(x+d)(x+g)(x-d) = 0$$
Since 2 is nonzero, each factor can equal zero:
  • $(x-d) = 0 \Rightarrow x = d$ (appears with multiplicity 2)
  • $(x+d) = 0 \Rightarrow x = -d$
  • $(x+g) = 0 \Rightarrow x = -g$
When counting distinct x-intercepts, we count each distinct $x$-value once, regardless of multiplicity.

steps.title: Verify all roots are distinct
steps.content:
Are the three values $d$, $-d$, and $-g$ all different? Given $d > 0$, $g > 0$, and $d eq g$:
  • $d > 0$ while $-d < 0$, so $d eq -d$
  • $d > 0$ while $-g < 0$, so $d eq -g$
  • Since $d eq g$, we have $-d eq -g$
All three roots are distinct, so there are 3 distinct x-intercepts. That matches choice B — B is correct.
